Off Calendar May-09-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Matthew M. Proudfoot, Esq. of Gates, O'Doherty 949/753-0255, appearing for defendants /moving parties Cherokee Camper Sales, Inc. and Tiffin Motor Homes, Inc.
Travis R. Jack, Esq., 661/250-1496, for plaintiff and opposing party

RULING:
Argued and TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED WITH MODIFICATION OF order striking award of sanctions: Defendant's motion to quash subpoena for production of business records is granted. The requested information is protected by the attorney-client and the attorney-work product privileges. The Court finds that plaintiff persisted over objection and without substantial justification in an attempt to obtain materials outside of the scope of permissible discovery. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
